meaning: adj.薄片的,成片的,薄而易剥落的,[美俚]极古怪的,与常人不同的
examples: He wondered if the idea wasn't just a little too flaky, a little too outlandish.
A sweep of chilly air passed by, rustling all the leaves and snowing the flaky ashes broadcast about the fire.
But in most browsers aside from IE it gets flaky.
The difference is that the flaky-currency players don't buy dollar-denominated bonds and don't buy longer-term paper.
Whole roasted fish (branzino or turbot) are reliably flaky and tender, served with caramelized lemon.
